title: Adam Chip Runtime Configuration
description: JSON Schema documenting all parameters in System/Config.json. This file is documentation only — it is not imported by code. Descriptions are in English to match code conventions.

agent.description: Core agent identity and runtime settings.

name.description: Display name of the agent persona. Used in logs and UI headers.

language.description: BCP-47 locale tag. Used by ASR and TTS services for language selection.

mode.description: Runtime mode. 'maintenance' disables wake word gate and power enforcement — use for development and debugging. 'exhibition' requires wake word 'адам' before ASR activates and enforces MAXN power mode.

data_dir.description: Absolute path to the agent data directory containing memory.sqlite3, events.jsonl, and inference_metrics.jsonl.

persona_paths.description: Relative paths to persona markdown files loaded into system prompt in order. Changing order affects prompt structure. Adding/removing files changes character depth.

prompt_trace_max.description: Maximum number of prompt trace entries kept in the in-memory ring buffer for the debug UI.

power.description: Jetson power management gate. Prevents exhibition startup on under-clocked hardware.

required_mode_name.description: Human-readable name of the required nvpmodel power mode.

required_mode_id.description: nvpmodel mode ID that must be active. 0 = MAXN (full power). Run 'nvpmodel -m 0' to set.

require_jetson_clocks.description: When true, orchestrator checks that jetson_clocks are active (all clocks at max frequency). Run 'jetson_clocks' to set.

enforce_in_exhibition.description: When true, orchestrator refuses to start in 'exhibition' mode unless power gate conditions are satisfied. Set to false only for development on non-Jetson hardware.

media.description: Video and audio input settings, including ESP32 fallback behavior and scene analysis cadence.

primary.description: Active video source. 'esp_mjpeg' uses ESP32 camera stream at esp_mjpeg_url (192.168.0.171:81/stream). 'jetson_gstreamer' uses local USB/CSI camera via GStreamer pipeline. 'remote_rtsp' uses an IP camera RTSP stream.

gstreamer_pipeline.description: GStreamer pipeline string used when primary='jetson_gstreamer'. Must produce RGB frames via appsink named 'adam_frames'.
gstreamer_pipeline.default: v4l2src device=/dev/video0 ! image/jpeg,framerate=30/1 ! jpegdec ! videoconvert ! video/x-raw,format=RGB ! appsink name=adam_frames drop=true max-buffers=1 sync=false

remote_rtsp_url.description: RTSP URL for remote camera. Used when primary='remote_rtsp'.

preview_enabled.description: When true, camera preview is served on the agent dashboard UI.

esp_mjpeg_url.description: HTTP URL of the ESP32 MJPEG camera stream.

esp_fail_threshold.description: Number of consecutive ESP32 camera fetch failures before falling back to jetson_gstreamer.

esp_retry_interval_sec.description: Seconds between ESP32 camera reconnection attempts after failure.

video_device.description: Linux video device path for local camera. Used by jetson_gstreamer pipeline.

camera_width.description: Capture width in pixels for local camera.

camera_height.description: Capture height in pixels for local camera.

camera_quality.description: JPEG quality (1-100) for camera capture. Higher = better quality but more data for VLM.

camera_capture_interval_sec.description: Interval in seconds between camera frame captures for VLM scene analysis.

audio.description: Audio input/output configuration and VAD parameters.

input_device.description: ALSA/PulseAudio device for microphone input. 'pulse' routes through PulseAudio (current production value). hw:1,0 (Jetson APE I2S) does not produce PCM data — do not use.

output_device.description: ALSA device for system audio output (not TTS — TTS uses services.tts.output_device).

sample_rate.description: Audio capture sample rate in Hz. Must match ASR service expectation (16000 Hz for WhisperX).

channels.description: Number of audio input channels. 1 = mono.

frame_ms.description: WebRTC VAD frame duration in milliseconds. Must be 10, 20, or 30 — WebRTC VAD requirement.

vad_threshold.description: Legacy RMS energy threshold. Kept for reference; active VAD is WebRTC VAD.

min_speech_ms.description: Minimum speech segment duration in milliseconds. Shorter segments are discarded.

max_segment_ms.description: Maximum recording segment length in milliseconds before forced submission.

mic_source.description: Active microphone source. 'esp32' fetches WAV clips from ESP32 HTTP API (/api/audio/clip). 'local' captures from input_device directly.

esp32_mic_profile.description: ESP32 microphone hardware profile name. Used by audio normalisation and format parsing.

esp_health.description: ESP32 microphone health monitor thresholds. Detects silent/clipping mic automatically.

silence_threshold.description: RMS level below which audio is considered silent (mic likely off).

ratio_threshold.description: Peak-to-RMS ratio above which audio is considered clipping.

clip_burst_threshold.description: Number of clipping frames in a burst before triggering health alert.

restore_threshold_polls.description: Number of healthy polls required before mic is considered restored.

webrtc_vad_aggressiveness.description: WebRTC VAD aggressiveness level. 0 = least aggressive (accepts more non-speech), 3 = most aggressive (filters most non-speech). 2 is the production tuning for exhibition distance.

max_command_segment_ms.description: Hard cap on LISTENING-phase command recording length in milliseconds. Prevents runaway recording from stuck VAD. REPLY phase uses reply_max_segment_ms instead.

reply_max_segment_ms.description: Hard cap on REPLY-phase command recording length in milliseconds. Reference logic specifies a shorter REPLY window than LISTENING (10s vs 15s) — REPLY is a follow-up turn without wake word, so the user's response is expected to be more concise.

normalize_factor.description: Target RMS level for audio normalisation before ASR submission.

spectrum_bands.description: Number of log-spaced frequency bands rendered in the chat-panel equaliser widget. Computed server-side from each audio frame's FFT (see _compute_bands in mic_reader.py). Emitted to clients as the `bands[]` field on audio_level events. 24 is the production default — wider bars are legible at typical UI sizes.

spectrum_min_hz.description: Lowest frequency (Hz) covered by the spectrum widget. Bands below this are not rendered. 80 Hz is approximately the fundamental of male speech; below that the ESP32 INMP441 has limited response anyway.

spectrum_max_hz.description: Highest frequency (Hz) covered. Capped at Nyquist (sample_rate / 2). At 16 kHz sample rate, max useful = 8000 Hz.

spectrum_floor_db.description: Per-band dBFS value mapped to bar height 0. Energies below this are rendered as zero. -60 dB is the conventional noise floor for visualisation.

spectrum_ceiling_db.description: Per-band dBFS value mapped to bar height 1.0 (full-scale). 0 dB = digital full scale. Bars at this level read solid red.

spectrum_cadence_hz.description: Target emission rate (Hz) for audio_level events carrying spectrum bands. Backend derives the per-Nth-frame counter from this and frame_ms — at sample_rate=16000, frame_ms=20, value 25 emits every 2nd frame. Higher cadence = smoother widget, more events.jsonl bytes (~10 KB/s at 25 Hz).

spectrum_color_yellow_at.description: Per-bar normalised level at which the colour transitions from green to yellow. Below this value bars render green→yellow gradient; above it they render yellow→red.

spectrum_color_red_at.description: Per-bar normalised level at which the colour reaches solid red. Bars above this are clipped/peaking — useful visual cue for input gain calibration.

events_jsonl_sample_audio_level.description: Writing-side sampler for high-frequency audio_level events in data/adam/events.jsonl. N means 'write every Nth event to disk' — at default 5 with 25 Hz spectrum cadence and bands[24] payload, on-disk pressure drops from ~36 MB/hour to ~7 MB/hour. SSE subscribers and the in-memory recent-events deque are NOT affected — every audio_level event is still broadcast at full cadence, so the chat-panel EQ widget keeps its 25 Hz responsiveness. Set to 1 to revert to legacy 1:1 logging (full forensic completeness, no disk-side compression). Set to 25 for maximum compression (~1 Hz effective on-disk cadence). Mitigation for RESEARCH §5/§12 (Phase 21A CONTEXT D-04).

scene_worker_enabled.description: Enable background VLM scene analysis worker. When false, no scene descriptions are generated and the LLM receives no visual context.

scene_interval_sec.description: Seconds between automatic VLM scene captures. Lower = more responsive scene awareness but higher VLM load.

scene_stale_after_sec.description: Seconds after which a scene description is considered stale and omitted from the LLM prompt.

scene_buffer_maxlen.description: Maximum number of scene descriptions kept in the rolling buffer.

scene_context_count.description: Number of recent scene descriptions injected into the LLM prompt. Higher = richer visual context but longer prompt.

services.description: External inference service endpoints and parameters.

llm.description: LLM service configuration (llama.cpp via OpenAI-compatible API).

provider.description: API provider type. 'openai' uses the OpenAI-compatible REST API (llama.cpp serves this at port 8081).

base_url.description: Base URL for the LLM API endpoint.

model.description: Model identifier sent in API requests. llama.cpp ignores this field and uses the loaded model regardless. The .gguf suffix mismatch between this value and the server's reported name is expected and harmless.

model_path.description: Relative path to the GGUF model file. Used by the systemd service unit to locate the model at startup.

timeout_sec.description: HTTP timeout for LLM API requests in seconds. Gemma 4 E4B takes ~9s for full prefill due to SWA cache reset.

temperature.description: LLM sampling temperature. Higher = more creative/varied responses, lower = more deterministic.

max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens in LLM response. Keep low (40-80) to stay in TTS latency budget. Longer responses noticeably delay TTS start.

num_ctx.description: KV cache context window size in tokens. Gemma 4 E4B resets the SWA cache between turns, so the full context is prefilled each time (~2781 tokens → ~9s).

vlm.description: VLM service configuration (VILA 1.5-3b via nano_llm Docker).

base_url.description: Base URL for the VLM API endpoint.

model.description: HuggingFace model ID for the VLM service.

timeout_sec.description: HTTP timeout for VLM API requests in seconds.

max_new_tokens.description: Maximum new tokens in VLM scene description. 80 recommended for two-part Scene+Engagement format. Higher values increase inference latency on Jetson.

prompt.description: Scene analysis prompt sent to the VLM with each frame. English only — VILA 1.5-3b produces better output in English. Supports exhibition-aware two-part format: 'Scene: [count+position]. Engagement: [level].' When a previous scene exists, Python appends 'Previous observation:' for delta context automatically.
prompt.default:
You are a fixed camera sensor in an interactive art installation. Describe in this format:
"Scene: [count+position]. Engagement: [none/watching/approaching/leaving/interacting]."
If empty: "Scene: empty room. Engagement: none."
English only.

asr.description: ASR service configuration (WhisperX CUDA via Docker, port 8095).

base_url.description: Base URL for the ASR API endpoint.

model.description: WhisperX model size. 'small' balances accuracy and latency on Jetson (~1-2s for short utterances). 'medium' is more accurate but ~2x slower.

language.description: BCP-47 language code for ASR. Must match the language the agent listens in.

listening_silence_timeout_sec.description: Seconds of silence in LISTENING phase (after wake word fires, before any speech is detected) before voice_loop returns to STANDBY. Reference logic = 6s. Protects against spurious wake-word detections — if OWW fires on noise but no one speaks, Adam returns to STANDBY instead of hanging in LISTENING forever. Replaces wake_word.wake_silence_timeout_sec (deprecated alias). Once speech is detected, silence_after_speech_ms takes over for end-of-utterance detection.

silence_after_speech_ms.description: How many consecutive milliseconds of silence after detected speech are required to mark the user's request as finished and forward it to ASR. Applies in both LISTENING (after wake word) and REPLY (after Adam's response) modes. Lower = faster Adam reaction but cuts off slow speakers; higher = more patient but introduces delay.

silence_rms_threshold.description: RMS audio-level threshold below which a frame is treated as silence regardless of what WebRTC VAD says. Acts as a secondary filter against constant background hum/HVAC noise that VAD may classify as voiced. 0 disables this filter (WebRTC VAD decides alone). Typical 200–500 for normal indoor rooms; raise for noisy exhibition halls. Compare with media.audio.normalize_factor (8000) — threshold should stay well below that.

reply_window_sec.description: Soft reply window in seconds (used by dialog-level no-reply heuristic in _set_voice_state('standby','no_reply')). Distinct from reply_silence_timeout_sec which is the in-loop VAD silence timer.

post_tts_discard_window_ms.description: Milliseconds after TTS playback finishes during which MicReader continues to drain the ESP32 socket but does NOT queue chunks to the consumer. This flushes the ESP32's internal stream buffer of self-echo audio (Adam's own voice picked up by INMP441 across the room) before the REPLY window starts accepting user speech. Reference observation: ESP32 stream lag is ~2300ms — chunks read at t=T contain audio captured at t=T-2.3s. Without this window, ~2 seconds of TTS-tail audio enters the queue right after mic_unmuted and gets transcribed as user speech in REPLY. Default 2500ms covers the worst observed lag with a small safety margin. Set to 0 to disable (only safe in setups with negligible mic→speaker acoustic coupling).

reply_silence_timeout_sec.description: Single silence timer for REPLY mode. After voice_state transitions to 'reply' and the post-TTS guard (_REPLY_GUARD_SEC=0.6) elapses, if no qualifying speech has accumulated (speech_ms == 0) for this many seconds, voice loop returns to STANDBY. Runaway-dictation protection in REPLY is provided by media.audio.reply_max_segment_ms (10s by reference logic), not by a separate hard cutoff.

endpointing_debounce_frames.description: Phase 9 (REQ-VAD-DEBOUNCE): minimum number of CONSECUTIVE silence-classified VAD frames (at frame_ms granularity, default 20 ms) required after speech before emitting the `endpointing_started` event. WebRTC VAD flickers voiced↔silenced at 20 ms granularity on tail syllables, quiet consonants, and far-field audio; without debounce a single user utterance can emit 20-40 `endpointing_started` events. Default 5 frames ≈ 100 ms — enough to filter sub-100 ms transients without delaying real end-of-speech detection.

endpointing_voiced_debounce_frames.description: Phase 9.1 (REQ-VAD-DEBOUNCE follow-up): symmetric voiced-side debounce. After endpointing_started latches, this many CONSECUTIVE voiced frames (default 3 ≈ 60 ms) are required to release the latch and allow the next silence run to emit again. Without this counter, a single voiced flicker frame (breath/click) immediately re-opens emission, producing 20-40 emissions per utterance even with silence-side debounce active (observed in Phase 9 Test 3: max 32 emissions per utterance). Does NOT affect ASR submission timing — silence_ms still resets on any voiced frame, so end-of-speech detection latency is unchanged. Diagnostic/logging concern only.

sample_rate.description: Audio sample rate in Hz sent to the ASR service. Must match the service expectation (16000 Hz for WhisperX).

timeout_sec.description: HTTP timeout for ASR API requests in seconds.

wake_words.description: Comma-separated wake word(s). The ASR service uses this list to gate transcription in exhibition mode.

wake_word_required.description: When true, ASR will not activate unless the wake word was detected by OpenWakeWord. In maintenance mode this is bypassed regardless.

reply_window_expired_action.description: Action when the reply window expires (soft or hard cutoff with no qualifying speech). 'standby' (default) — return voice loop to STANDBY, keep mic running, ready for next wake word. 'stop' — fully stop the voice loop (mic off, no OWW); requires explicit /api/voice/start to resume. Use 'stop' for exhibition power-saving when long idle periods are expected.

disable_local_fallback.description: When true (default and the only supported value in V-S08.1+), MicReader retries the ESP32 stream forever with `esp_retry_backoff_sec`. The legacy local-fallback cascade was removed in Phase 11 — ESP32 INMP441 is the only sanctioned mic. Maintenance-mode debugging on hosts without ESP uses mic_source="local" instead.

esp_open_timeout_sec.description: Per-attempt timeout in seconds for opening the ESP32 :81 WAV stream (`_NO_PROXY_OPENER.open(url, None, timeout)`). Replaces the legacy hardcoded 30 s. Lower value (8) trades a slightly higher false-failure rate during ESP boot for faster recovery cycles — the retry loop in MicReader (`esp_retry_backoff_sec`) is the canonical recovery mechanism.

esp_probe_after_fails.description: After this many consecutive `:81` stream-open failures, MicReader probes `:80 /api/status` (cheap, ~150 ms) before attempting another `:81` open. If the probe fails, MicReader skips the `:81` attempt and sleeps the next `esp_retry_backoff_sec` slot. Saves roughly `esp_open_timeout_sec * remaining_attempts` seconds when ESP is fully down. 0 disables probing (always retry :81).

esp_retry_backoff_sec.description: Exponential-backoff sequence (seconds) between MicReader stream-reopen attempts. Index = `min(consecutive_fail_count - 1, len - 1)`; the final element is reused indefinitely once exhausted. Default `[2, 4, 8, 15]` gives recovery under 5 s for transient drops (REQ-RECOVERY-UNDER-5SEC) while capping reconnect storms at one every 15 s. List MUST contain at least one positive number; otherwise MicReader busy-loops on failure.

esp_stream_restart_after_fails.description: Phase 21A-08 watchdog: after this many consecutive `:81/audio` open failures, MicReader auto-sends `POST :80/api/system/stream/restart` to break ESP firmware's stale-socket deadlock. ESP firmware's `:81` web-server has only 4 stream slots (CLAUDE.md gotcha); when keepalive-deaths leave them in zombie state, normal retry backoff cannot recover — only a `:81` restart frees them. Default 5 means roughly 30-45 sec of regular retries before triggering (depends on `esp_retry_backoff_sec`). The restart fires ONLY when `_probe_esp_status` returns OK (control plane on `:80` is alive) — if ESP is fully dead, restart is wasted. Cooldown is enforced via `esp_stream_restart_cooldown_sec`. Set to 0 to disable the watchdog entirely (legacy behavior).

esp_stream_restart_cooldown_sec.description: Phase 21A-08 watchdog: minimum interval (seconds) between two consecutive auto-restart triggers. Prevents restart-loop when ESP firmware takes longer than `esp_retry_backoff_sec` window to bring `:81` server back up — without this gate, fails climb to threshold again before previous restart finished settling. Default 120 sec (2 min) is comfortable for the ~8-15 sec ESP web-stack reboot plus a safety margin. Lower values (60 sec) can be used during exhibition for faster recovery at the risk of restart spam; higher values (300 sec) for conservative maintenance-mode operation.

tts.description: TTS service configuration (Silero v5_5_ru HTTP service, port 8082).

base_url.description: Base URL for the TTS API endpoint.

speaker.description: Active Silero voice ID. Must be one of the available_speakers values.

sample_rate.description: TTS audio sample rate in Hz. Must match Silero model output (24000 for v5_5_ru). Do not change without rebuilding the TTS service — mismatched sample rate causes pitch shift.

timeout_sec.description: HTTP timeout for TTS synthesis requests in seconds.

available_speakers.description: Available Silero TTS voice IDs for the v5_5_ru model.

output_target.description: Where to play synthesized TTS audio. 'jetson_hdmi' (default) — local aplay/paplay through services.tts.output_device. 'esp32_speaker' — POST WAV to mcu.speaker_url; Jetson resamples 24000 Hz Silero output to 44100 Hz before sending (ESP32 PCM5102A expects mono 16-bit 44100). No automatic fallback to HDMI on ESP32 errors — failed playback returns ok=False.

output_device.description: ALSA device for local TTS playback when output_target='jetson_hdmi'. 'plughw:1,3' = HDMI audio (card 1, HDA NVIDIA). hw:1,0 (Jetson APE I2S) does not produce PCM output — do not use. Ignored when output_target='esp32_speaker'.

filler_enabled.description: When true, TTS plays a short filler phrase (e.g. 'Хм...') while the LLM is generating, to reduce perceived silence latency.

filler_phrase.description: Text of the filler phrase spoken while LLM generates response.

filler_delay_ms.description: Milliseconds after LLM request before filler phrase is spoken. Prevents filler on fast responses.

filler_probability.description: Probability (0.0..1.0) that the filler phrase is actually spoken once filler_delay_ms has elapsed. 0.0 = effectively disabled, 1.0 = always play (legacy behavior). The dice are rolled per turn independently; no cooldown. Lowering this from 1.0 makes Adam feel less talky / less repetitive.

mcu.description: ESP32-S3 MCU (motor controller) HTTP API configuration.

base_url.description: Base URL for the ESP32 HTTP API (port 80). Static IP set via W5500 Ethernet.

speaker_url.description: URL for the ESP32 speaker playback endpoint (port 81). Separate server on the ESP32 handles audio streaming.

timeout_sec.description: HTTP timeout for MCU API requests in seconds. Keep low — MCU commands are fire-and-forget.

idle_scene.description: Default motor scene played when agent enters standby.

allowed_scenes.description: Whitelist of scene names accepted by the action layer. Any scene name from the LLM not in this list is rejected as no_action.

max.description: Maximum valid PWM channel index (PCA9685 has 16 channels: 0-15).

value_max.description: Maximum valid PWM value (full on, 12-bit resolution).

sounds.description: Local audio cue configuration for Jetson-side system sounds.

enabled.description: Master switch for local sound cues (startup chime, error sounds).

success_path.description: Relative path to the startup success sound file played after orchestrator initialisation.

local_output_device.description: ALSA device used for local cue playback (not TTS).

esp_boot_note.description: Informational note only — not a config value. Documents that the ESP boot sound is embedded in firmware.
esp_boot_note.default: ESP boot sound is embedded in ESP firmware and played by ESP after speaker init

error_path.description: Relative path to the error sound file played on critical failures.

safety.description: Motor safety constraints enforced by the action layer before any MCU command is sent.

motor_default_duration_ms.description: Default duration in milliseconds for motor activation commands that do not specify a duration.

motor_max_duration_ms.description: Hard cap on motor activation duration in milliseconds. Any LLM-requested duration above this is clamped. Prevents mechanical damage from runaway activations.

motor_cooldown_ms.description: Minimum milliseconds between consecutive motor commands to prevent mechanical stress.

half_duplex_mute.description: When true, microphone input is muted during TTS playback to prevent acoustic echo feedback. MUST remain true in all production modes.

wake_word.description: OpenWakeWord ONNX wake word detection configuration.

model_path.description: Relative path to the ONNX wake word model file. The model is trained for the Russian word 'адам'.

threshold.description: Detection confidence threshold (0.0-1.0). 0.10 is tuned for current room acoustics with debounce_hits=2 preventing single-frame false positives. Lower → more sensitive but more false positives. Increase if too many false triggers; decrease if the agent misses activations.

debounce_hits.description: Number of consecutive audio frames above threshold required to confirm wake word. Prevents spurious triggers from single-frame noise spikes.

vad_threshold.description: VAD pre-filter threshold for wake word detection. 0 disables VAD pre-filtering.

wake_silence_timeout_sec.description: DEPRECATED — moved to services.asr.listening_silence_timeout_sec in Phase 11. The timer is semantically part of the ASR pipeline (it counts silence within LISTENING phase), not the wake-word engine. Reader prefers the new key; this one is kept as a fallback for backwards compatibility with old Config.json files.

tuning.description: Runtime persona/behavior parameters — hot-reloadable, editable from WebUI tuning panel via /api/tuning. Previously lived in Agent Adam Chip/Tuning.json; merged here so Config.json is the single source of truth. Validated by pydantic models in System/adam/tuning.py.

memory.description: Episodic + semantic + recent-injection memory parameters and the dawn consolidator job schedule. Editing these affects what Adam remembers across sessions and which past interactions get injected into future prompts.

episodic.description: Episodic memory: per-session salience scoring and decay. Each completed session is scored 0..1; anything above salience_threshold becomes a durable episode.

enabled.description: Master switch for episodic memory writes.

salience_threshold.description: Minimum salience score to persist a session as an episode. Lower = more episodes saved (noisier memory).

decay_days.description: Days after which episode weight starts decaying toward zero in retrieval.

duration_normalize_seconds.description: Reference session duration (sec) used to normalise the duration weight in salience scoring.

weights.description: Salience scoring weights — MUST sum to 1.0 ± 0.01 (validated by pydantic).

introduced_name.description: Weight for episodes where the user introduced their name.

duration.description: Weight for session length (normalised by duration_normalize_seconds).

themes.description: Weight for the number of distinct themes (from memory.theme_clusters) touched in the session.

tone.description: Weight for emotional/tonal intensity of the session.

echoes_used.description: Weight for how many echo entries Adam used in his replies.

new_question.description: Weight for whether the user asked a previously-unseen question.

highlights_max_per_episode.description: Max number of conversation highlights captured per episode.

recurring_min_visits.description: Minimum number of past visits before a user is considered 'recurring' (different greeting / memory injection).

recurring_lookup_days.description: Window (days) within which past visits count toward recurring status.

semantic.description: Semantic memory: free-form notes accumulated by the consolidator.

enabled.description: Whether semantic notes are injected into prompts.

max_chars.description: Hard cap on semantic-memory bytes injected per prompt.

recent_injection.description: How to surface recent episodes back into Adam's working context.

enabled.description: Whether to inject recent episodes at all.

limit.description: Max recent episodes injected per turn.

strategy.description: Retrieval strategy: match by user's name, by detected theme, or either.

max_age_days.description: Don't inject episodes older than this.

consolidator.description: Daily consolidator cron (Engineering/consolidator.py) — compresses raw episodes into semantic notes overnight.

enabled.description: Master switch for the consolidator job.

model.description: LLM model override for consolidation; null = use the default from services.llm.

window_start.description: Earliest local time the job is allowed to run (HH:MM).

window_end.description: Latest local time the job is allowed to run (HH:MM).

max_episodes_per_run.description: Cap on episodes processed in one consolidation pass.

temperature.description: LLM temperature for the consolidation prompts.

max_runtime_minutes.description: Hard timeout for one consolidation run.

retry_on_invalid_patch.description: If the LLM returns malformed semantic patch JSON, retry once with stricter prompt.

gate_log_max_days.description: How long to keep the consolidator gate log around (decision audit trail).

instant_threshold.description: Episodes with salience above this run through instant consolidation instead of waiting for the nightly job.

theme_clusters.description: Theme name → list of trigger keywords. Used by episodic scoring (themes weight) and recent_injection (by_theme strategy). Add new themes to expand what Adam recognises across sessions.

echoes.description: Pool of pre-written 'echo' utterances Adam can drop into replies (e.g. fragments of memories). Tag-matched against the current conversation.

global_cooldown_turns.description: Min turns between ANY two echoes firing.

per_echo_cooldown_days.description: Min days before the SAME echo can fire again.

match_threshold.description: Min tag-match score for an echo to be eligible.

weight_multiplier.description: Global multiplier on echo selection weights.

matcher_type.description: Matching algorithm: tag-set overlap or TF-IDF cosine.

score_boost.description: Bonus added to score when an echo's tags exactly match.

tag_short_cutoff.description: Tag sets shorter than this get a different scoring formula (over-weight short matches less).

default_entry_weight.description: Default weight for echo entries that don't declare their own.

chinese.description: Chinese-language interjections — same machinery as echoes but for the bilingual flavour layer. Rarer, longer cooldown.

global_cooldown_turns.description: Min turns between any two Chinese interjections.

per_echo_cooldown_days.description: Min days before the same Chinese phrase repeats.

match_threshold.description: Higher than echoes — Chinese should only fire on clear topical matches.

audio_mode.description: How Chinese phrases get played: pre-rendered audio file, audio with TTS fallback, or text-only (Silero TTS pronounces transliteration).

session.description: Session-end detection logic — when does Adam consider the visitor 'gone'.

end_strategy.description: Which signal ends a session: silence on VAD, no face for N seconds, both (combined), idle with a 'are you there?' grace message, or an explicit external event.

vad_silence_seconds.description: Seconds of mic silence before considering session over.

face_lost_seconds.description: Seconds without a detected face before considering session over.

grace_message.description: Phrase Adam says during idle_with_grace before fully ending the session.

scene_director.description: Motor-scene director — orchestrates ESP32 motor scenes (idle / alternating / etc.) based on conversation state.

enabled.description: Master switch for the scene director.

sustain_seconds.description: Minimum seconds a scene runs before director can switch it.

cooldown_between_changes_seconds.description: Min seconds between consecutive scene switches.

hysteresis_seconds.description: Hysteresis window — director won't flip back to a recently-left scene within this many seconds.

override_priority_scenes.description: Scene names that bypass sustain/cooldown when triggered.

llm.description: Runtime LLM tuning. NOTE: services.llm.temperature/max_tokens carry the *infrastructure default*; these tuning values override per-turn behavior.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for replies. Higher = more varied, lower = more deterministic.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on tokens per reply. Lower keeps TTS latency tight.

response_word_target.description: Soft target words per reply — used in the prompt to nudge Adam toward concise replies.

voice.description: Runtime voice tuning. NOTE: services.tts.speaker carries the infrastructure default; this overrides for the active session.

speaker.description: Active Silero speaker id (must be in services.tts.available_speakers).

speed_multiplier.description: Playback speed (1.0 = original Silero output rate).

volume.description: Output volume gain applied before the WAV reaches ALSA / ESP32.

prompt.description: Prompt-builder runtime knobs. NOTE: agent.history_turns is a separate ceiling; tuning.prompt.history_turns is the *effective* value used per turn.

history_turns.description: Dialogue turns included in the LLM prompt.

include_scene.description: Whether VLM scene descriptions get injected into the prompt.

include_sensors.description: Whether ESP32 sensor readings (light, motion) get injected into the prompt.

diagnostics.description: Runtime diagnostics — toggles for verbose output without restarting services.

log_level.description: Effective log level for adam.* loggers.

metrics_enabled.description: Whether per-turn timing metrics are recorded.

trace_prompts.description: When true, captures full system+user prompts into the ring buffer (visible under /metrics in WebUI). Heavy — only enable for debugging.

trace_post_tts_lag.description: When true, MicReader emits a mic_lag_diag_chunk event for every drained chunk during 4 s after each post-TTS mic_unmute. Use with scripts/diag_lag_source.py to plot the RMS envelope and identify whether the ~2.3 s tail-of-Adam echo originates in ALSA HDMI buffer drain, room acoustic reverb, or the ESP32 firmware's internal audio FIFO. Heavy — ~200 events per turn.
